message:
fix(cli,repair): harden evidence ledger against non-UTF8 torn writes and fix log_add_exp edge cases

The evidence ledger reader used `BufRead::lines()` which returns an error
on non-UTF8 byte sequences. Under power-loss or torn-write conditions the
ledger file can contain partial/malformed byte sequences at the tail. This
caused the entire ledger load to fail, preventing repair operations from
accessing any historical evidence records.

Switch to manual `read_until(b'\n')` with an explicit `from_utf8` check so
that malformed lines are silently skipped while all well-formed JSON records
are still loaded. This matches the existing lenient behavior for unparseable
JSON lines (which are already silently skipped via the `Ok(record)` pattern).

Also fix `log_add_exp` in the autopilot Bayesian repair scheduler to properly
handle NaN propagation and +Infinity inputs. The previous implementation used
`!is_finite()` checks which conflated NaN, +Infinity, and -Infinity. Now:
- NaN input → NaN output (IEEE 754 propagation)
- +Infinity input → +Infinity output (correct identity)
- -Infinity input → return the other operand (log-space zero)

Changes:
- crates/ffs-cli/src/main.rs: Replace lines() with read_until() loop,
  add from_utf8 guard, add test for non-UTF8 line skipping
- crates/ffs-repair/src/autopilot.rs: Rewrite log_add_exp with explicit
  NaN/+Infinity/-Infinity case handling

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
